authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes the Food Code as the official model food-safety code for retail food establishments, making it authoritative for grocery food-safety terminology, customer complaint caveats, and escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety-sensitive intake, prepared-food complaint triage, temperature-control terminology, and safety escalation
used_for: prepared-food and perishable complaint evidence expectations
boundary between service response and food-safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls, Market Withdrawals, & Safety Alerts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal regulator for most foods and publishes official recall and safety-alert references that constrain customer-service communication about recalled or unsafe grocery products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall inquiry, lot-code review, customer communication, product hold, and quality or food-safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this rule guide governs order shipment representations, delay notices, cancellation options, prompt refunds, and transaction recordkeeping for online or telephone merchandise orders, including grocery e-commerce where applicable.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: online grocery order intake, delivery-delay communication, cancellation option handling, refund status, and fulfillment evidence review
used_for: customer communication caveats for delayed or unfulfilled orders

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 233 - Guides Against Deceptive Pricing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC pricing guides are federal consumer-protection guidance for advertised savings, comparative pricing, and sale representations that directly constrain grocery shelf-tag, circular, loyalty, BOGO, and digital-promotion communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: shelf-tag mismatch, circular price dispute, loyalty offer issue, digital coupon issue, and promotion-exception escalation
used_for: source conflict handling for price-adjustment cases

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Examination Procedure for Price Verification
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Handbook 130 is a recognized legal-metrology reference used by weights-and-measures programs; its price-verification procedure directly addresses UPC scanner, PLU, manual-entry, shelf-price, advertised-price, and receipt-price mismatch evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price verification, scanner error intake, PLU or weighted-item mismatch, shelf tag review, and receipt evidence packaging
used_for: UPC, PLU, shelf-tag, and receipt artifact expectations
boundary between service explanation and pricing authority

authoritative_sources.title: SNAP EBT Factsheet for New Retailers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FNS administers SNAP retailer authorization and EBT transaction rules; this official guidance establishes EBT POS processing, exact purchase amount, transaction record, and retailer-help boundaries for benefit-tender complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SNAP EBT tender issue, split-tender order review, refund-status communication, and processor handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to duplicate grocery charges, refund-not-posted complaints, and tender disputes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, duplicate charge, refund-not-posted complaint, and issuer handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect refund rerouting, loyalty account updates, delivery address changes, and online grocery account recovery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch handling and fraud-sensitive grocery support escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and this FAQ directly clarifies call-center cardholder-data handling boundaries that affect grocery support requests involving payment cards and payment credentials.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data handling in contact-center, chat, or service-desk-assisted grocery support

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, customer identity, store or order context, receipt or loyalty references, tender type, item facts, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, safety-sensitive, fraud-sensitive, or owned by another lane.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, receipt, item, promotion, and tender record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate order state, pickup or delivery status, POS receipt lines, UPC, PLU, scale weight, shelf tag, circular, promotion, loyalty offer, coupon, substitution, inventory state, refund status, and payment tender evidence.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Supported grocery service resolution
workflow_stages.description: Resolve status, explanation, documentation, missing-item, substitution, order-readiness, promotion-application, refund-status, or service-ticket follow-up requests when records and delegated policy support direct response.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer communication and expectation control
workflow_stages.description: Prepare safe customer-facing summaries that distinguish documented facts, tenant-policy application, remaining blockers, and next owner without overstating refund, price-adjustment, safety, or fraud authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Policy-exception, repeat-failure, and payment-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported refund, price-adjustment, loyalty credit, rain-check, delivery credit, split-tender, SNAP EBT, repeat-failure, or source-conflict cases into explicit escalation packets with evidence and owner routing.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Food-safety, recall, fraud, and payment-security stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported direct resolution and escalate whenever illness, recall, temperature-control, allergen, suspicious identity, refund reroute, card-data, EBT PIN, or loss-prevention signals change the safe answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: order numbers, pickup or delivery windows, curbside handoff logs, delivery proof, substitution preferences, picker notes, shorted-item records, and order-management status
POS receipts, receipt line numbers, tender records, refund status, split-tender details, SNAP EBT transaction references, gift-card or store-credit records, and payment gateway references
UPC, SKU, PLU, scale weight, weighted-item labels, shelf tags, circulars, digital coupons, loyalty offers, BOGO offers, rain-check records, price files, and promotion rules
inventory states, out-of-stock records, department notes, cold-chain or temperature-control notes, prepared-food production references, lot codes, best-by dates, and recall notices
CRM notes, service tickets, customer contact history, complaint chronology, service recovery matrix, approval notes, and repeat-failure records
food-safety, quality, incident, illness, allergen, recall, fraud, loss-prevention, chargeback, and account-takeover case references
systems_of_record: SNAP EBT or WIC processor portal where applicable

allowed: Classify grocery requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, informational, exception-driven, complaint-driven, safety-sensitive, fraud-sensitive, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, store or channel context, and jurisdiction facts needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status explanations, customer communication summaries, service dispositions, and next-step packages without overstating authority.
Apply documented service rules for supported refunds, substitutions, missing items, order status, promotion explanation, and service follow-up when tenant policy and records support direct response.
Prepare escalation packets and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required customer, order, POS receipt, promotion, inventory, payment, refund, EBT, recall, food-safety, CRM, or loss-prevention records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, service-recovery approval, SNAP/WIC eligibility interpretation, food-safety signoff, recall disposition, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or loss-prevention action.
Illness, recalled product, allergen, temperature-control, security, suspicious identity, refund reroute, card-data, EBT PIN, or account-takeover signals change whether direct response is safe.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, store, jurisdiction, supplier, processor, SNAP/WIC, promotion, recall, or local-health policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise or apply a refund, price correction, delivery credit, substitution, rain check, loyalty adjustment, or goodwill compensation that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, promotion, refund, SNAP/WIC, food-safety, recall, fraud, or loss-prevention controls without explicit authority.
Ask for or restate full payment-card data, EBT PINs, account credentials, or other restricted payment credentials in insecure communication channels.
Declare a food product safe, a recalled item cleared, an illness complaint resolved, or a suspicious account event benign without designated owner signoff.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a refund, price adjustment, delivery credit, substitution exception, rain check, loyalty credit, or goodwill offer when the receipt, order, tender, promotion, or approval map does not support it
a shelf-tag, circular, loyalty, digital coupon, UPC, PLU, or scale-weight mismatch is handled as generic appeasement instead of being tied to price-verification and promotion evidence
a split-tender, SNAP EBT, refund-not-posted, duplicate charge, or chargeback complaint is resolved without payment processor or issuer boundary awareness
a prepared-food, allergen, temperature-control, recall, lot-code, or illness complaint is treated as routine service recovery instead of food-safety or quality escalation
repeat missing-item, missed-delivery, or failed-coupon contacts are treated as isolated apology cases instead of escalation-worthy service breakdowns
fraud, account-takeover, refund-reroute, coupon abuse, or loss-prevention signals are ignored to satisfy a customer request
the industry overlay collapses into generic customer-service wording instead of using grocery order, POS, shelf-tag, PLU, tender, substitution, cold-chain, recall, and loyalty terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local scanner-price, item-pricing, refund, rain-check, bottle-deposit, SNAP/WIC, and retail-food-code adoption rules can materially narrow the customer-facing answer beyond the federal or model baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, store, SNAP/WIC processor, and tenant policy context before applying a specific customer outcome. No blocking ambiguity remains because the lane escalates unresolved conflicts rather than guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Recall notices, illness complaints, allergen concerns, and local health-department instructions may require supplier, quality, legal, or food-safety owner review before outbound messaging or refunds are finalized.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records the ambiguity and routes to designated food-safety, quality, legal, or operations owners. Direct resolution is limited to supported service communication and evidence packaging.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of generic retail customer-service scripting.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdictional, tenant, store, processor, supplier, local-health, and recall differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at grocery customer-service resolution, communication, and escalation packaging. Pricing overrides, discretionary refund approval, SNAP/WIC interpretation, food-safety decisions, fraud findings, and loss-prevention action remain outside the lane.
